_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.7 NCL Bliss Alaska Solo Group pack. Built fresh 2026-06-11 during the careful-not-clever pass + content boardroom (triad, 2-vendor). Primary-source verification: Wikipedia (Norwegian Bliss), sitkadock.com (terminal's own site), mmtoursofjuneau.com (operator's own price page), NCL hull-art blog + NCLH press history, Icy Strait Point port guides + ziprider.com, Goldbelt Tram retail listings, ca.usembassy.gov. No claim cites another voyage pack (Copy-Propagation rule) — where batch packs shared facts (NCL gratuity tiers, ship-phone relay), the underlying primary sources are cited, not the sibling packs.
verification_session: Full read of all 998 pre-fix lines, then primary-source verification of every suspect claim, then factual corrections, then voice pass, then content boardroom (Positioning Sage + Direct-Response Veteran + Offer Architect, each on two vendors blind), then adopted-content execution. Sailing is 30 days out (Jul 11 2026) — the highest-urgency pack in the catalog.

decks.value: 20 decks (pack-cited; Wikipedia article does not enumerate; consistent with Breakaway Plus deck plans)
decks.notes: Flag for precision check against NCL deck-plan PDF before any wider distribution.

capacity_double_max.notes: CORRECTED from pack's prior '~4,004 double / ~4,888 max'. Wikipedia primary cites 4,002 double; the max figure was removed pending a primary source.

crew.notes: CORRECTED from pack's prior '~1,716'. Wikipedia cites 1,700.

year_built.notes: Delivery date precision added to pack body (was 'April 2018').

class.value: Breakaway Plus — third of four (Escape 2015, Joy 2017, Bliss 2018, Encore 2019)

hull_art.value: Humpback-whale mural by marine-life artist Wyland (the 'Whaling Walls' muralist). NCL has carried hull art since Norwegian Dawn (2002) and named-artist murals since Peter Max on Norwegian Breakaway (2013), then Guy Harvey on Escape (2015). Bliss is the line's only Wyland.
hull_art.notes: MAJOR CORRECTION: pack previously claimed 'Bliss was the first NCL ship with full hull artwork' — false. Also removed the unverified '270-foot' whale dimension. Both At-a-Glance and Section 1 corrected to the attributed history.

godparent.value: Elvis Duran (radio host) — christened Norwegian Bliss in Seattle, May 30, 2018.
godparent.notes: CORRECTION 2026-07-06: the original sidecar claimed 'Wikipedia does not name the christening godmother' — false. Wikipedia names Elvis Duran (May 30, 2018, Seattle). The pack body asserts no godparent (no shipped error), but the sidecar's stated verification limit was wrong; corrected.

date.value: Delivered April 21, 2018; christening ceremony details not verified this pass and not asserted in pack body.

auto_gratuity_rate.value: $20/person/day standard cabins; $25/person/day Suites & Haven. 7-night totals $140 / $175. Not adjustable onboard; written post-cruise refund requests only. 20% auto-gratuity on beverages, spa, specialty dining.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: Pack body Section 8 + Day 8 figures consistent.

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry on Breakaway Plus ships; full-service per-item or bag-special only.
laundry_availability.source: NCL published amenities for Breakaway Plus class
laundry_availability.notes: Pack body does not currently include a laundry subsection; sidecar records for completeness. Optional future addition.

ship_phone_relay.value: 1-888-627-4477 — NCL's paid ship-phone relay (credit card required, per-minute rate). NCL publishes no free 24/7 family-emergency desk.
ship_phone_relay.notes: CORRECTION: pack previously carried '+1 (305) 436-4000 / +1 (866) 234-0292' as 'NCL Care Team' — numbers that do not match NCL's published relay and were flagged unverified in an earlier batch audit. Handoff card now carries the State Dept OCS line (+1-888-407-4747 / +1-202-501-4444, verified via ca.usembassy.gov consular-resources page this session) as the emergency path and the verified NCL relay for non-emergency messages.

drink_package_pricing.value: Premium Beverage Package ~$109/day + 20% gratuity pre-cruise if not bundled via Free at Sea; breakeven ~5-6 drinks/day.

sitka_docking.claim: Bliss docks at the Sitka Sound Cruise Terminal (Halibut Point), ~5 miles north of downtown. FREE continuous shuttle to downtown (330 Harbor Drive), 10-15 min ride, reservation-managed on busy days (reservation.sitkadock.com), ADA-accessible vehicles, last shuttle leaves downtown 30 minutes before all-aboard.
sitka_docking.disposition: MAJOR CORRECTION — pack previously claimed Sitka was a tender port ('the ship anchors; tenders run... 20-30 minute end-to-end tender process'). Wrong: large ships dock at SSCT. Four locations corrected (arrival block, walkable-list header, transit note, accessibility section). NOTE: the v0.1.4 Anthem pack's '$9-12 round-trip shuttle' claim is ALSO invalidated by this primary source — flagged in followups.

icy_strait_point_docking.claim: Bliss docks at Icy Strait Point — no tendering. Two berths: Adventure Landing (floating pier) and Wilderness Landing (deep-water dock built by Huna Totem Corporation in partnership with NCL, ~$17M, purpose-built to eliminate tendering for mega-ships). Free Transporter Gondola (4-5 min, enclosed, step-free) connects Wilderness Landing to the cannery area; distinct from the paid SkyGlider (~$49.95).
icy_strait_point_docking.disposition: MAJOR CORRECTION — pack previously claimed ISP was a tender port. Also corrected the Day 5 arrival/departure prose ('mid-morning/evening') to match the itinerary table (6:00 AM - 3:00 PM), an internal inconsistency.

route_open_pacific.claim: Sitka is an outer-coast port (Pacific side of Baranof Island). The Seattle-Sitka leg (Day 1 evening - Day 2 overnight) runs the open Pacific west of Vancouver Island — the most exposed stretch of the week. Mid-itinerary (Sitka-Juneau-Endicott-ISP-Ketchikan) is protected inside waters. The Ketchikan-Victoria leg returns to open Pacific, entering the Strait of Juan de Fuca for the Victoria approach.
route_open_pacific.source: Route geography established in the batch's Anthem research pass (Seattle round-trips transit open Pacific west of Vancouver Island; Inside Passage protection begins at the BC/Alaska channels); Sitka's outer-coast position per NPS and chart geography.
route_open_pacific.disposition: CORRECTION — pack previously claimed 'Most of the route is sheltered... briefly' and named the 'Strait of Georgia' for the Day 7 approach (wrong strait — that is the inside channel toward Vancouver city; Victoria is approached via the Strait of Juan de Fuca). Same error class fixed on the Anthem pack. Day 2 itinerary label, Day 2 section, and Section 7 seasickness all corrected.

pvsa_victoria.claim: Passenger Vessel Services Act of 1886 requires foreign-flag ships (Bliss is Bahamian-flagged) to call at a foreign port between U.S. ports; Victoria is the nearest foreign port to Seattle. The pack previously used the colloquial-but-wrong 'Jones Act' label (the Jones Act covers cargo).
pvsa_victoria.source: https://www.govinfo.gov (46 U.S.C. § 55103, PVSA) + the Anthem pack's Round-2 boardroom verification of the same mechanism
pvsa_victoria.disposition: CORRECTED at both locations + full disclosure paragraph added per the batch's Anthem precedent.

mendenhall_glacier_express.claim: M&M Tours 'Glacier Express': $79 adult / $49 child, ~3 hours total, includes round-trip transport + Mendenhall visitor-center admission, 2 hours at the glacier. The old 'Blue Bus' shuttle-only product no longer exists.
mendenhall_glacier_express.disposition: Day-by-day was already correct at $79; the BUDGET TABLE said '$25 (Blue Bus)' and the savings line said 'saves $50' — both corrected. Mid-range budget line replaced the infeasible 'Mendenhall + whale combo $220' (7+ hours of touring against 6.5 ashore) with a feasible Glacier Express + Mount Roberts tram pairing.

mount_roberts_tramway.claim: $65 adult all-day pass (2026); Goldbelt sells day passes, not round-trip-only tickets; base station at the cruise dock.
mount_roberts_tramway.disposition: CORRECTED from pack's $60. NOTE: the Anthem pack's '$45 r/t' figure is also outdated — flagged in followups.

ziprider.claim: Icy Strait Point ZipRider: 5,330 ft long, ~1,300 ft vertical drop, $199 walk-up; cruise-line bookings range ~$150-269.
ziprider.disposition: Day 5 prose was correct at $199; budget 'How it costs more' line said '~$179' — corrected.

sitka_excursion_prices.claim: Sea otter & whale tour ~$210-225 (True Alaskan Tours ~$210); Fortress of the Bear $20 adult self-guided, packaged tours $50-80; Russian Bishop's House free (NPS); The RIDE Sitka $5 day pass / $2 one-way.
sitka_excursion_prices.source: Pack-cited operator listings, retained from the pack's original research (dated 2026-05-17); spot-consistent with current listings
sitka_excursion_prices.disposition: Budget table's '$9 (transit bus)' and '$180 (sea otter tour)' corrected to match the day-by-day's verified figures.

butchart_gardens.claim: CA$44.25 adult summer admission incl. tax (top of the seasonal range CA$32.75-44.25); evening programming Wed-Sun in July/August; ~$60 round-trip transport from Ogden Point; ~$105 USD all-in.

us_consulate_vancouver.claim: U.S. Consulate General Vancouver emergency line +1-604-685-4311 (covers Victoria BC); State Dept OCS 24/7: +1-888-407-4747 (US/Canada), +1-202-501-4444 (abroad).

ketchikan_ward_cove.claim: NCL ships berth at Ward Cove, ~7 miles north of downtown Ketchikan, with a complimentary continuous shuttle (20-30 min) to Berth 4 downtown. NCL christened the Ward Cove facility alongside the ISP pier investment.
ketchikan_ward_cove.disposition: Pack's Ward Cove framing confirmed accurate.

handoff_card_itinerary.claim: Correct port order: Seattle → Sitka → Juneau (+ Dawes Glacier) → Icy Strait Point → Ketchikan → Victoria BC → Seattle.
handoff_card_itinerary.source: Pack's own verified itinerary table (gangwaze/cruisemapper cross-checked per pack sources)
handoff_card_itinerary.disposition: CORRECTED — the family handoff card listed 'Juneau → Icy Strait Point → Sitka', contradicting the itinerary table. A wrong port order in the family's emergency reference card was the single most consequential internal inconsistency in the pack.

worlds_largest_ziprider.claim: 'World's Largest ZipRider' — operator's own marketing name; pack attributes it and notes the 'Largest' framing explicitly.

observation_lounge_largest.claim: Pack now frames as 'what the line billed at her debut as the largest observation lounge in its fleet' — attributed to NCL's marketing at delivery rather than asserted as current fact.

hull_art_first.claim: (REMOVED) 'Bliss was the first NCL ship with full hull artwork' — false; replaced with the attributed lineage (Dawn 2002, Peter Max 2013, Guy Harvey 2015, Wyland on Bliss).

ketchikan_totem_collection.claim: 'World's largest collection of standing totem poles' split between Saxman and Totem Bight — long-standing Ketchikan tourism claim, retained with the split-location framing that keeps it accurate.
ketchikan_totem_collection.source: Ketchikan visitor bureau materials; NPS Totem Heritage Center context
ketchikan_totem_collection.disposition: retained, attributed to the destination's own claim

machine_tells.antithetical_stacking_notes: 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown timer' (Welcome) — within the single-instance triplet cap; brand signature.

templated_section_headers.notes: 'Honest read on [port]' x5 (Sitka, Juneau, ISP, Ketchikan, Victoria) varied to port-specific framings, same fix pattern as the six prior batch packs. The seasickness 'The honest read:' was absorbed into the route-honesty rewrite.

single_most_best_superlative_frequency.notes: Port Valet 'single most useful piece' varied to the luggage-friction framing, matching the Anthem fix.

once_in_a_lifetime_frequency.notes: ZipRider instance retained (single use); the 'For first-timers' duplicate trimmed.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING in the editor's voice — but the pack carries Kristie's attributed lived experience (3x Alaska, quoted from her own host card), which is the strongest attributed-lived anchor in the batch. The new Welcome attestation note names the boundary explicitly: Kristie's experience is hers and quoted as hers; everything else is primary-source research.

examples: Welcome attestation note (what's first-hand vs researched)
Cagney's-vs-glacier collision tradeoff spelled out with no pretended verdict
'Questions worth asking Kristie' defers to the person who has stood on these decks
Gray Line evening schedule 'being finalized — verify'
Sitka shuttle reservation system + last-shuttle warning

from_the_pier_specificity.verdict: strong — SSCT free-shuttle system with reservation URL and last-shuttle rule, Ward Cove shuttle reality, Wilderness Landing + Transporter Gondola, Glacier Express door-to-door timing, the 1:30 PM Juneau all-aboard against tour durations, Pier 66 vs Pier 91 distinction, Link light rail routing

named_real_specifics.verdict: present — operators (M&M, Harv & Marv's, TEMSCO, True Alaskan, Island Wings, Carlin Air, Misty Fjords Air), venues, the named host with her attributed card details, Currant Bistro/Sound Hotel, Biscuit Bitch Belltown with the closed-sister-location note

signals: Kristie's attributed lived detail (Diet Dr Pepper, smiley faces, 3x Alaska) — the batch's strongest attributed-personal anchor
the Cagney's/glacier collision called out as a real unresolved tradeoff
primary-source docking corrections visible in the prose (free shuttle + reservation system + last-shuttle rule)
route honesty against the 'Inside Passage' brand
infeasible-combo warning (glacier + whales don't fit 6.5 hours)
the group's real composition (~8 members, US + Australia, widowed/solo by life-stage)
named closed-location note (Biscuit Bitch downtown sister)
shared planning sheet as living document

cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Zero Layer 1 signals post-fix; Layer 2 within allowances; 14 Layer 3 counter-signals including the batch's best attributed-lived material (the host's own card). The pack's authority shape is curator + quoted-veteran-host + primary-source operator data.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no urgency. Group-schedule framing is informational.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: All crutch counts at or below batch thresholds; the five templated port headers varied; major factual landmines (two false tender claims, false hull-art first, wrong strait, wrong handoff-card port order, four budget-table contradictions) corrected against primary sources; boardroom-adopted social-layer content is corpus-native and contains no fabricated experience.
recommended_followup_edits: ANTHEM CROSS-FIX REQUIRED: the v0.1.4 Anthem pack still claims the Sitka terminal shuttle is '$9-12 round-trip' (invalidated by sitkadock.com — the shuttle is free, reservation-managed) and 'Mount Roberts Tramway $45 r/t' (now $65 all-day). Two-line fix + sidecar note in the Anthem pack.

Upgrade the 'Questions worth asking Kristie' block with her actual answers if she provides them before sale — the DR-Veteran boardroom lens rated host-specific insight the single highest-value addition, and her real answers would convert it from prompt to content.
Verify Bliss deck count (20) against NCL's deck-plan PDF.
Christening godparent never verified — add only if a primary source is found.
Optional: a short laundry subsection (no self-service on Breakaway Plus; the bag-special) for parity with sibling packs.
Operator hand-edit pass planned (the pack should read as substantively the operator's).

_note_2026_07_06_adversarial_fix: Adversarial audit (2026-07-06): 1 MAJOR + minors, fixed. (1) Sitka 'the ONLY Russian Orthodox cathedral in North America' is false (it is the FIRST/oldest; many exist) -> 'first'. Same error family as the Anthem pack. (2) Sidecar wrongly said Wikipedia doesn't name the Bliss godmother -> it names Elvis Duran (christened Seattle May 30 2018); corrected. MINOR real-money: Seattle Link light rail $3.50 -> $3.00 flat (fare change Aug 2024). MINOR Hoonah/Icy Strait Point population ~750 -> ~930 (2020 census). All ship specs, dates, phones, gratuity remain verified correct; the DUBIOUS Goldbelt/Mount-Roberts-Tram operating-status flag is a pre-sailing verify item.
